app: add g4occt interactive Geant4 session executable (#314)

* app: add g4occt interactive Geant4 session executable

Adds a standalone `g4occt` executable that starts a Geant4 session
with a dynamic detector construction driven by /G4OCCT/ messenger
commands and positional CLI arguments.

## CLI

  g4occt [-m macro] [-u UIsession] [-t nThreads] [file.step ...] [file.xml ...]

- `-m`, `-u`, `-t` mirror the B4c example options (no `-vDefault`).
- Positional `.step`/`.stp` files are queued as geometry; solid vs
  assembly is auto-detected via XDE free-shape count.
- Positional `.xml` files are loaded as G4OCCT material-map XMLs.

## Messenger commands (/G4OCCT/)

Three G4GenericMessenger instances (one per sub-directory) are owned
by G4OCCTDetectorConstruction:

  /G4OCCT/load/step     <file>         load STEP (auto solid/assembly)
  /G4OCCT/load/assembly <file>         force XDE assembly import
  /G4OCCT/load/material <file>         load material-map XML
  /G4OCCT/world/setMaterial <name>     world material (default G4_AIR)
  /G4OCCT/world/setSize <x> <y> <z>    world half-size
  /G4OCCT/vis/open [driver]            open vis driver (default OGL)
  /G4OCCT/vis/scene                    redraw + refresh scene

GUI menus (File/Run/View) are defined in gui.mac and picked up
automatically by the Qt session.

## World sizing

If no explicit size is set, Construct() pads the bounding box of all
placed solids/assemblies by 10 %.  An empty world defaults to 1 m³.

## Tests

Three CTest integration tests (run_g4occt_empty, run_g4occt_solid,
run_g4occt_cmd_load) verify exit-code 0 under BUILD_TESTING=ON.

## AGENTS.md

Documents the new src/app/ convention and G4GenericMessenger as the
preferred messenger pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: install macros to DATADIR per FHS, add macro search path

Per the Linux Filesystem Hierarchy Standard, architecture-independent
application data belongs in ${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATADIR}/<package>, not
next to the executable in BINDIR.

Changes:
- Move GNUInstallDirs include before app add_subdirectory so
  CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_DATADIR is available in the app CMakeLists.txt
- Install g4occt macros to ${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATADIR}/g4occt/macros/
- Copy macros into build-tree macros/ subdirectory (mirrors install layout)
- Add G4OCCTAppConfig.hh.in template with both INSTALL and BUILD macro dirs
- main.cc: call G4UImanager::SetMacroSearchPath with both paths so macros
  are found whether running from the build tree or after installation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: replace configure_file header with target_compile_definitions

Instead of generating G4OCCTAppConfig.hh.in via configure_file,
pass the macro directory paths directly as preprocessor definitions:

  G4OCCT_MACRO_DIR_INSTALL  (installed share/g4occt/macros)
  G4OCCT_MACRO_DIR_BUILD    (build-tree macros/ subdirectory)

This avoids a generated header and keeps the .hh extension reserved
for real header files, while achieving the same run-time search-path

commit.message:
Fix view containment/equality and scalar proxy resolution bugs

KeysView.__contains__: return False for non-string keys instead of
raising TypeError, matching Python dict_keys behavior.

ItemsView.__contains__: return False for non-tuples and tuples with
non-string first element instead of raising TypeError, matching Python
dict_items behavior.

ItemsView.__eq__: iterate with plain Python values and check
containment instead of building a PySet, fixing TypeError on
unhashable values (lists, dicts) and matching Python dict_items
set-based equality semantics.

ScalarProxy.__contains__: resolve proxy value argument before calling
operator.contains, so `proxy in string_proxy` works.

ScalarProxy.__pow__/__rpow__: resolve proxy modulo argument before
calling builtins.pow, so `pow(proxy, n, proxy)` works.

commit.message:
Fix five bugs: underflow, tzinfo equality, AoT validation, pop double-borrow, view equality

  underflow in debug builds when deleting a slice from an empty array.
  Add Rust unit test exercising the empty-array path.

- equality: TOML local time must not compare equal to a timezone-aware
  Python time. Check py_time.get_tzinfo() before field comparison.

- item_ops: reject non-table values in AoT single-index assignment.
  Previously the scalar was silently dropped on serialization.

- dict_proxy: resolve proxy keys before borrow_mut in pop(), preventing
  a double-borrow panic when a ScalarItem is used as the key.

- views: ItemsView.__eq__ now uses set semantics (delegates to PySet.eq),
  matching Python's dict_items. ValuesView.__eq__ removed — Python's
  dict_values has no equality support, falling back to identity.

commit.message:
Fix and test: dollar sign in filename is escaped but not in path prefix during tab completion (#345)

When completing a path like `$HOME/foo/<TAB>` where a file named
`$baz.txt` exists, the `$` in the prefix (`$HOME`) must not be
backslash-escaped — only the `$` in the newly resolved filename should
be escaped.

Expected: `$HOME/foo/\$baz.txt`  
Not: `\$HOME/foo/\$baz.txt`

## Changes

- **`docker/tab_completions.Dockerfile`**: Adds `/root/foo/$baz.txt`
test fixture (a file with a literal `$` in its name under `$HOME/foo/`)
- **`tests/completion_util.sh`**: Adds `--env-var-test` completion
handler that returns `$HOME/foo/$baz.txt` directly in `COMPREPLY`,
exercising the programmable completion quoting path
- **`src/app/tab_completion.rs`**: Adds `run_test_on` case asserting
`fl_comp_util --env-var-test $HOME/foo/` → `$HOME/foo/\$baz.txt`

## Fix

The bug was in the programmable completion path
(`post_process_single_completion`). When
`filename_quoting_desired=true`, the old code called
`quote_function_rust` on the entire suggestion string, which incorrectly
escaped the `$` in the user-typed prefix `$HOME`.

`post_process_single_completion` now receives `word_under_cursor` and a
pre-computed `dequoted_word_prefix`. When quoting, if the suggestion
starts with the dequoted prefix (the part the user already typed), that
prefix is preserved verbatim from `word_under_cursor` and only the
newly-discovered suffix is quoted. The dequoting is computed once in
`post_process_completions` and shared across all suggestions to avoid
redundant work.

The secondary-completion path (`tab_complete_glob_expansion` via
`PathPatternExpansion`) already handled this correctly and is
unaffected.

commit.message:
Fix double-borrow panics when using proxy keys, and harden array mutation

Using a ScalarItem proxy as a key/index in __setitem__, __delitem__, or
pop() triggered a double-borrow panic: the mutable borrow was taken
before extracting the key, and CPython's __index__ protocol on the proxy
re-borrowed the document.

Fix by resolving proxy keys via resolve_proxy() before borrow_mut(),
matching the existing pattern for value comparisons.  Also fix
require_str_key's error handler which called extract::<i64>() (triggers
__index__) — replaced with a C-level is_instance_of check.

Additionally:
- array_setitem_slice and item_extend (Array branch) now validate all
  values up front via into_value before mutating, mirroring the AoT
  paths fixed in fe27ac7.
- apply_update_pairs checks key existence before set_with_decor_preservation
  rather than after (where the key always exists).

commit.message:
install: use master branch, replace existing flyline enable lines in bashrc (#356)

Branch references used `main` throughout; the default branch is
`master`. The bashrc setup logic only checked for an exact command match
and appended a duplicate if the path changed — it should detect and
replace any existing flyline enable line instead.

## Changes

- **`install.sh` / `README.md`**: `main` → `master` in all raw/blob
GitHub URLs
- **`install.sh` bashrc logic**: replace exact-match `grep -qF` +
append-only with pattern-based detection and in-place replacement:
- Detects `^enable flyline` and `^enable -f <path> flyline` (handles
bare enable, old path, or inline comment)
- Replaces matching lines via `sed -i -E` rather than appending a
duplicate
- Falls back to append only when no existing flyline enable line is
found

```sh
# Before: only matched if exact command was already present
grep -qF "$ENABLE_CMD" "$BASHRC"

# After: matches any existing flyline enable form and replaces it
grep -qE '^enable( -f [^ ]*)? flyline( |$)' "$BASHRC"
sed -i -E "s|^enable( -f [^ ]*)? flyline( .*)?$|${ENABLE_CMD}|" "$BASHRC"
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

> improve the install script. 
> the branch it gets from should be master not main. search for other
times I mention main instead of master.

commit.message:
Add Alpine/Ubuntu install test images and end-to-end release pipeline validation (#357)

Adds two Docker images that test the full `install.sh` installation
pipeline against a real release, and wires them into the release
workflow. Also updates `install.sh` to accept a pinned version via env
var instead of always resolving `latest`.

## Changes

### `install.sh`
- Checks `FLYLINE_RELEASE_VERSION` env var; if set, fetches from
`releases/tags/$VERSION` instead of `releases/latest`
- Validates the API response on tag lookup to surface a clear error if
the version doesn't exist

### New Dockerfiles
- `docker/install_test_alpine.Dockerfile` — Alpine + `gcc bash curl`,
runs `install.sh | sh`, verifies with `bash -i -c "flyline --version"`
- `docker/install_test_ubuntu.Dockerfile` — Ubuntu + `curl`, same
install/verify pattern

### `docker/docker-bake.hcl`
- Added `variable "FLYLINE_RELEASE_VERSION"` (null default,
env-overridable)
- Added `install-test-alpine` and `install-test-ubuntu` targets passing
the variable as a build arg

### Release workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`)
- `publish-release` job: runs after all matrix builds, marks the release
as `--prerelease --draft=false`
- `test-installation` job: runs after `publish-release`, builds both
install-test images with `FLYLINE_RELEASE_VERSION=${{
needs.create-release.outputs.version }}` — exercising the real
download-and-enable path against the just-published artifacts

commit.message:
Cache Playwright browser binaries in CI (#5693)

`playwright-install` downloads browser binaries (~7 min) on every run.
Cache them.

- Cache `~/.cache/ms-playwright` via `actions/cache@v4`, keyed on
`runner.os` + Playwright version
- On cache hit: skip browser download, only run `npx playwright
install-deps` for system libraries
- On cache miss: existing behavior (`npx playwright install
--with-deps`)
- Cache auto-invalidates on Playwright version bumps
- Refactored the `playwright-install` action to only handle caching +
install (removed `setup-node`/`npm ci` to avoid duplication since
callers already have these)
- Migrated `js.yml` and `perf-smoke.yml` from inline `npx playwright
install` to use the cached action, ensuring all Playwright workflows
benefit from caching

viz: improve ray-tracer render quality (dark bg, zoom, brighter solid) (#309)

* viz: improve ray-tracer render quality (dark bg, zoom, brighter solid)

Three visual improvements to render_geometry_fixtures, none touching
G4TheRayTracer itself:

1. Dark background (0.12, 0.12, 0.16) instead of the default white.
   Provides high contrast with the steel-blue solid and a more
   professional / documentation-quality appearance.

2. ViewSpan zoom: multiply the auto-computed FOV by kViewSpanZoomFactor

   so the solid previously occupied only ~35% of the image height.  A
   0.80 factor brings it to ~60% with comfortable margins and no

fixtures locally and inspecting the JPEG output.

commit.message:
perf: multi-ray BVH voting in Inside() to reduce BRepClass3d fallback rate (#305)

* perf: multi-ray BVH voting in Inside() to reduce BRepClass3d fallback rate

Replace the degenerate/zero-crossing fallbacks in Inside() Tier-2a with
a 3-ray majority vote (+Z primary, +X and +Y on ambiguity). Non-degenerate
rays vote by parity; degenerate rays abstain. A clear majority returns the
result without touching BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier. Tie or all-degenerate
still falls back to the exact classifier (extremely rare).

The near-surface guard (bvhLB < tolerance) is preserved unchanged to
ensure correctness when the tessellation error band could flip the result.

Local benchmark results (5 repetitions, Release build):
  Fixture                            main     this PR   speedup
  direct-primitives/g4cons           32.3 ms   7.1 ms   4.6×
  direct-primitives/g4sphere         23.1 ms   6.5 ms   3.6×
  direct-primitives/g4torus          56.7 ms  15.3 ms   3.7×

Curved solids benefit most because their triangulated surfaces produce
many degenerate or zero-crossing rays along the primary +Z direction,
all of which previously fell back to BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform().
The fallback now triggers only on genuine manifold discontinuities.

commit.message:
Fix: DaviesBouldinScore returns NaN for invalid cluster counts (#3699)

`DaviesBouldinScore` propagated sklearn's `ValueError` when the number
of unique labels was `< 2` or `>= n_samples`. This mirrors the fix
already applied to `CalinskiHarabaszScore`.

## Changes

- **`davies_bouldin_score.py`**: Added pre-call guard in
`_davies_bouldin_score` that returns `float("nan")` when
`n_unique_labels < 2` or `n_unique_labels >= n_samples`, matching the
`_calinski_harabasz_score` implementation exactly. Updated docstring to
document NaN behavior.
- **`test_davies_bouldin_score.py`**: Added
`test_invalid_cluster_count_returns_nan` parametrized over both invalid
cases (single cluster, every sample its own cluster), mirroring the
equivalent test in `test_calinski_harabasz_score.py`.

```python
metric = DaviesBouldinScore()
metric.update((torch.randn(10, 5), torch.zeros(10, dtype=torch.long)))  # single cluster
result = metric.compute()  # previously raised ValueError, now returns float("nan")
```

commit.message:
Add GHA workflow for Copilot documentation consistency checks (#353)

Adds a GitHub Actions workflow that runs Copilot CLI on push to `master`
to verify documentation stays consistent with the source code. If
inconsistencies are found, it commits fixes to a branch and opens a PR
with a summary.

### New files

- **`.github/workflows/copilot-docs-check.yml`** — Installs
`@github/copilot`, runs it against the repo, and creates/updates a PR on
`copilot/docs-check` if anything changed. Follows the same checkout →
branch → force-push → `gh pr create || gh pr edit` pattern as
`update-settings-docs.yml`.
- **`.github/prompts/docs-check.prompt.md`** — Extracted prompt
instructing Copilot to cross-check README.md, clap doc comments in
`src/lib.rs`, and `examples/` scripts against actual code (default
values, keybindings, flag names, feature descriptions). Source code is
treated as source of truth; only docs are modified.

### Setup required

A fine-grained PAT with the **Copilot Requests** permission must be
stored as the `COPILOT_PAT` repository secret.

commit.message:
fix: edit modal save closes window and shows save feedback (#20)

The save button in the edit modal had no error boundary — if
`updateProduct` or `renderProductList` threw (e.g. localStorage quota
exceeded), `cleanup()` was never reached and the modal stayed open.
There was also no confirmation that the save succeeded.

## Changes

- **`showToast(message, type)`** — new helper that appends a temporary
`role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` div at the top of the screen,
fades out after 3 s
- **`onSave` wrapped in try/catch:**
- Success → `cleanup()` closes modal, then green `✅ Produkt gespeichert`
toast
- Error → inline `edit-error-msg` shows `"Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte
versuche es erneut."`, modal stays open for retry

```ts
try {
  updateProduct(product.id, { name, expiryDate, notifyDaysBefore });
  renderProductList(container);
  checkAndNotify();
  cleanup();
  showToast('✅ Produkt gespeichert');
} catch {
  if (errorMsg) errorMsg.textContent = 'Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte versuche es erneut.';
}
```

save](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14683926-a0ae-4c6a-bfa2-729bd1a79b5d)

commit.message:
Fix wall collision: replace pixel-drift model with target-tile movement

Pac-Man and ghosts now move tile-to-tile toward explicit target tile centers.
Wall checks happen before setting the next target tile, so it is impossible
for either entity to enter a wall tile.

Add Pac-Man game

Complete client-side Pac-Man game built with HTML5 Canvas, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript.

Features:
- 60FPS game loop with requestAnimationFrame
- 4 ghosts with BFS pathfinding (Chase/Scatter/Frightened/Eaten modes)
- Power pellets, dot collection, lives system
- Scoring: dots=10, pellets=50, ghosts=200/400/800/1600
- Web Audio API sound effects (no external files)
- Keyboard (Arrow/WASD) + touch swipe + D-pad controls
- Mobile responsive canvas scaling
- 2 levels with increasing difficulty

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix notification timezone bug and add product edit functionality (#18)

- [x] Fix notification date parsing bug in `notifications.ts` (use local
time like `formatDate` by appending `'T00:00:00'`)
- [x] Fix DST fall-back bug: change `Math.ceil` → `Math.round` in
`getDaysUntilExpiry` so notifications trigger correctly across DST
transitions
- [x] Add `updateProduct` function to `storage.ts` (resets `notified`
flag when date/days change)
- [x] Add ✏️ edit button to each product card in `app.ts`
- [x] Add `showEditModal` function with pre-populated form and
validation error messages
- [x] Add edit modal HTML with name, date, notify-days fields,
Save/Cancel buttons, and error message
- [x] Add info note below notification button about browser/OS
limitations (app must be open; iOS Safari partial support)
- [x] Remove accidentally committed `dev-dist/` artifact and add to
`.gitignore`
